What This Stage Did
In Plain English
Draws the actual metal wires connecting every cell. First global routing plans which tracks to use, then detailed routing assigns exact metal layer segments and vias. DRC violations must be zero.
Output: A fully routed DEF file. Zero DRC violations = ready for GDSII export.
Quality of Results (QOR)
TNS ✗
-75.230ns
Total Negative Slack — sum of how much every late path is late by. Smaller magnitude = healthier design.
WNS ⚠
-1.710ns
Worst Negative Slack — the single most-violated timing path.
Sequential power ℹ
1.8mW (20.9%)
Flip-flop and latch power. Internal + switching + leakage.
Combinational power ℹ
5.6mW (64.4%)
Logic gate power. Internal + switching + leakage.
Clock power ℹ
1.3mW (14.7%)
Clock-tree buffers and inverters.
Macro power ℹ
0.0mW (0.0%)
Hard IP / SRAM macros.
Total power ℹ
8.7mW
Total estimated power for this stage.
DRC violations ✓
0
Design Rule Check violations. MUST be 0 for fabrication. Any DRC = foundry rejects.
Via count ✓
29vias
Connections between metal layers. More vias = more resistance.
Antenna violations ⚠
1
Long metal antennas can damage gate oxide during fab. Fixed by inserting diodes.
Layout Screenshot
What To Do Next
→ DRC violations must be 0. Run ondevtra inspect route.
→ Try DIODE_INSERTION_STRATEGY=5 for antenna violations.
→ Reduce PL_TARGET_DENSITY and re-run the full flow.
Paste into terminal: ondevtra ask '...'
